name: Random/Complex Payload
description: Packet with diverse payload bytes testing all byte value ranges
purpose: General correctness verification: algorithm works for arbitrary patterns

crc_coverage.description: CRC calculated over Header through Payload (16-byte diverse payload)

payload_details.pattern_description: Mix of edge cases and frame bytes

coverage.note: Pattern designed to catch bit-manipulation errors in CRC

crc_output.note: Values will be populated by firmware execution

expected_complete_packet.description: Full packet including CRC and footer
expected_complete_packet.structure: Header(2) + Seq(1) + Cmd(1) + Len(2) + Payload(16) + CRC(2) + Footer(1)
expected_complete_packet.note: Bytes 22-23 will contain CRC output, byte 24 will be footer 0xAA

verification.method: Execute firmware CRC16::Calculate() on packet_before_crc bytes
verification.procedure:
1. Run firmware test harness
2. Execute CRC16::Calculate([complete packet_before_crc], 22)
3. Capture the returned u16 value
4. Transmit complete packet (including CRC and footer 0xAA)
5. Capture transmitted bytes from serial/USB
6. Record actual crc_value and transmitted_crc_bytes
verification.notes: This is a TEMPLATE, not a canonical vector. Canonical status pending firmware execution.

testing_notes: Verifies CRC algorithm handles diverse byte values correctly
Tests algorithm robustness with frame header bytes in payload
Confirms no false positives (frame header bytes don't break sync)
Validates bit-level CRC operations on edge cases
Ensures algorithm is not sensitive to specific data patterns

interoperability.requirement: Every implementation must produce identical complete packet bytes
interoperability.note: Byte-for-byte match required, not just CRC values
